text.title: Required sections in ui-ux-design.md
text.summary: Six sections in fixed order. The fillable template is provided in templates/ui-ux-design.md.tmpl.

list.item: Intent — what the user is trying to accomplish, in one sentence.
Layout — coarse description of element placement; a screenshot or wireframe is allowed but not required.
States — table with one row per state: empty, loading, error, success, disabled. Each row describes the visible affordance.
Nielsen audit — five rows (N1, N3, N4, N5, N6) per content/02-nielsen-five.xml. Each row: heuristic, finding, fix-or-noted-OK.
Norman audit — two rows (affordance, feedback) per content/03-norman-principles.xml.
Copy & microcopy — exact strings for button labels, error messages, empty-state text, success messages.

rule.statement: The six sections MUST appear in this order. Empty sections are filled with "n/a" and a reason, never omitted.
rule.rationale: Stable order + explicit n/a + reason makes the artefact diff-able across features.

section.title: The States table is load-bearing
section.p: Empty / loading / error / disabled are the states most often left unspecified, and they generate ~80% of post-launch UI bug reports. The table forces the author to name each one before code lands.

antipattern.description: States section with only "success" filled in.
antipattern.reason: The other four states will be improvised in code, inconsistently across pages.

text.reference: Fillable Markdown template with the six sections and audit-row stubs.
